steps.title: Identify what needs emphasis — the research's investigative goal
steps.content: The notes describe both Terada's question (wondering if Earth sends oxygen to the Moon) and his result (confirming it with Kaguya data). Emphasizing “the aim” means focusing on the investigative question that drove the research, not what was ultimately found.

steps.title: Find the choice that states the research's purpose directly
steps.content: C uses “set out to determine whether” to express the investigative goal that motivated the study. Choices A, B, and D focus on method (A), prior wondering (B), and result (D)—none emphasize the research's aim. This matches choice C — C is correct.
